title: RHCSA 10 Mock Exam H
description: A RHCSA 10 mock exam focused on RHEL 10 administration, Flatpak, systemd timers, storage, networking, users, security, and services.

tasks: Set hostname to clienth.exam10.lab and map serverh.exam10.lab to 192.168.122.3.
Configure System eth1 with IPv4 address 192.168.122.67/24, gateway 192.168.122.1, and DNS 192.168.122.3.
Activate the throughput-performance tuned profile.
Install lsof and ensure tcpdump is removed.
Use server as the only chrony source and enable chronyd.
Allow TCP port 8107 permanently in firewalld and reload.
Create enabled BaseOS and AppStream repository definitions using http://server/repo/BaseOS/ and http://server/repo/AppStream/ with GPG checks disabled.
Create system Flatpak remote examhflatpak pointing to file:///opt/rhcsa/flatpak/repo with GPG verification disabled.
Install org.rhcsa.Tools from examhflatpak, then remove it after verification.
Create group teamh10, create user userh10, set password cinder9, and add the user to teamh10.
Allow %teamh10 to run /usr/bin/systemctl without a password by using a sudoers drop-in.
Set maximum password age for userh10 to 52 days and warning period to 7 days.
Create /usr/local/bin/h-who that prints the primary group for the supplied user argument.
Write users whose shell ends with sh to /root/h-shell-users.txt.
Create gzip archive /root/h-etc.tar.gz containing /etc/hosts and /etc/fstab.
Create /root/h-original, hard link /root/h-hard, and symlink /root/h-soft.
Create and enable examhtimer.timer that runs every 10 minutes.
Create VG vgh10 and LV datah mounted at /mnt/datah10.
Persistently enable httpd_can_network_connect.
Configure persistent systemd journal storage.
Create a cron job for userh10 that writes EXAM10 to /home/userh10/exam10.log every 15 minutes.
Set the default target to multi-user.target without rebooting.
hints: Make changes persistent unless a question says otherwise.
Use local repositories and local documentation; do not depend on internet access.
Flatpak and systemd timer tasks belong to the RHCSA 10 track.
checks: connection_name="System eth1"; nmcli -g NAME connection show "$connection_name" >/dev/null 2>&1 || connection_name="$(private_dev="$(ip -o -4 addr show | awk '$4 ~ /^192\.168\.122\./ {print $2; exit}')"; nmcli -t -f NAME,DEVICE connection show --active | awk -F: -v private_dev="$private_dev" '$2 == private_dev {print $1; exit}')"; test -n "$connection_name" && nmcli -g ipv4.addresses connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx '192.168.122.67/24' && nmcli -g ipv4.gateway connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx '192.168.122.1' && nmcli -g ipv4.dns connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx '192.168.122.3' && nmcli -g ipv4.method connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx manual
grep -Eq '^server server iburst$' /etc/chrony.conf && systemctl is-enabled chronyd | grep -qx enabled
getent group teamh10 >/dev/null && id -nG userh10 | tr ' ' '\n' | grep -qx teamh10
systemctl cat examhtimer.timer | grep -Fqx 'OnCalendar=*:0/10' && systemctl is-enabled examhtimer.timer | grep -qx enabled
solution_outline: Work through each question independently and verify before moving on.

solution_commands: nmcli connection modify "System eth1" ipv4.addresses 192.168.122.67/24 ipv4.gateway 192.168.122.1 ipv4.dns 192.168.122.3 ipv4.method manual connection.autoconnect yes

cat > /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa10-exam.repo <<'EOF'
[rhcsa10-exam-baseos]
name=RHCSA10 Exam BaseOS
baseurl=http://server/repo/BaseOS/
enabled=1
gpgcheck=0

[rhcsa10-exam-appstream]
name=RHCSA10 Exam AppStream
baseurl=http://server/repo/AppStream/
enabled=1
gpgcheck=0
EOF
